candidates.name: Janet Mills
candidates.notes: 75th Governor of Maine (2019-). First woman governor of Maine. Former Attorney General. Mother was Congregationalist; personal faith not publicly confirmed. Progressive record: expanded Medicaid, signed gun control, protected abortion access.

profile.education: University of Massachusetts Boston (BA); University of Maine School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Attorney; served in Maine House of Representatives and as Maine Attorney General (twice); became Maine's first female governor and 75th governor in 2019.

candidates.name: Susan Collins
candidates.notes: Senior Senator from Maine (1997-). Most moderate Republican senator. Heritage Action lifetime 24%. NRA C rating (was B in 2020, declined). Voted to confirm Kavanaugh but also voted to acquit Trump and for Respect for Marriage Act. Catholic but breaks from church teaching on many social issues. Pro-choice, supports same-sex marriage.

profile.background: Former staffer for Senator William Cohen and executive director of the Center for Family Business at Husson University. First elected to Senate in 1996. Known as a centrist Republican.
profile.office: 413 Dirksen Senate Office Building

aipac-track.title: TrackAIPAC — Maine delegation
aipac-track.excerpt: Aggregates every AIPAC and pro-Israel-lobby contribution each member of Congress has received, sourced directly from FEC filings and OpenSecrets. Matches the RESOLUTE Citizen America-First question 3: foreign-backed lobby donations.

aipac-opensecrets.title: OpenSecrets — donor search for Susan Collins
aipac-opensecrets.excerpt: Non-partisan campaign-finance database. Search the candidate's name to see every FEC-reported donor, including pro-Israel lobby contributions.

aipac-endorsements.title: AIPAC Election Center — endorsed candidates
aipac-endorsements.excerpt: AIPAC's own public endorsement list. If a candidate appears here, they have accepted AIPAC backing; the RESOLUTE Citizen scoring criterion treats this as a failure on America-First question 3.

candidates.name: Angus King
candidates.notes: Independent Senator from Maine (2013-). Caucuses with Democrats. Attends St. Paul's Episcopal Church in Brunswick. Heritage Action lifetime 4%. NRA D rating. Former Governor of Maine (1995-2003). Describes himself as 'neither Democrat nor Republican, but an American.'

profile.education: Dartmouth College (BA), University of Virginia School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Independent who caucuses with Democrats. Former Governor of Maine (1995-2003). Energy company founder. Attorney and former aide to Senator William Hathaway. TV host.
profile.office: 133 Hart Senate Office Building

district_offices.address: 40 Western Ave, Room 412, E.S. Muskie Federal Building
